---
title: "MBYEXA001_TSHPAT016_Assignment3"
author: "Exauce Mbuyi"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
library(clue)
library(fpc)
library(patchwork)
library(mclust)
library(cluster)
library(factoextra)
library(kohonen)
library(colorspace)
library(readr)
library(viridis)
library(scales)
library(tidyverse)
library(GGally)
library(kableExtra)
library(knitr)
load("airbnbListings.RData")
```

\section{Data Wrangling & Part One}

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# First remove all missing valued objects:
clean_data <- na.omit(Airbnb_Excel_Lab_AAMB_Co)

# Variable renaming:
clean_data <- clean_data %>% 
  rename(
    ward_level = Neighbourhood_Cleansed,
    property_type = `Property_Type_Proper & Trim`,
    host_name = `Host_Name_Trimmed & Proper`,
    room_type = `Room_Type_Proper & Trim`,
    neighbourhood = `Neighbourhood_Proper & Trim`
         )

# Checking for variables with no variability in them:
length(unique(clean_data$room_type))
length(unique(clean_data$property_type))
length(unique(clean_data$host_name))
length(unique(clean_data$neighbourhood))

# Performing PCA for dimension reduction:
numeric_cols <- sapply(clean_data, is.numeric)
numeric_data <- clean_data[, numeric_cols] # Taking only the numerical variables

PCA_results <- prcomp(numeric_data, center = T, scale. = T)

  # Scree plot:
plot(PCA_results, type = 'l', main = "Scree Plot") 

  # Obtain first 4 loading vectors:
loadings_mat <- PCA_results$rotation[ , 1:4]

  # scatter plot matrix:
pairs(PCA_results$x[, 1:4], 
      col = rgb(0, 0, 0, 0.2),
      pch = 16, 
      main = "Scatterplot Matrix of Top 4 PCs")

# REMOVING OUTLIERS:

PC_scores <- PCA_results$x[, 1:4]
centroid <- colMeans(PC_scores)
cov_matrix <- cov(PC_scores)

mahal_dist <- mahalanobis(PC_scores, centroid, cov_matrix)

threshold <- qchisq(0.95, df = 4)
outlier_indices <- which(mahal_dist > threshold)

clean_data_no_outliers <- clean_data[-outlier_indices, ]
numeric_data_no_outliers <- numeric_data[-outlier_indices, ]

cat("Removed", length(outlier_indices), "outliers\n")

  # Create a new PCA outlier-free object
PCA_results_clean <- prcomp(numeric_data_no_outliers, center = T, scale. = T)

  # Creating new variables:
availability <- PCA_results_clean$x[ , 1]
review_score <- PCA_results_clean$x[ , 2]
listing_size <- PCA_results_clean$x[ , 3]
num_of_reviews <- PCA_results_clean$x[ , 4]
#identifier <- PCA_results_clean$x[ , 5]

  # Plot the outlier free scatter plot matrix:
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
pairs(PCA_results$x[, 1:4], col = rgb(0, 0, 0, 0.2), pch = 16, main = "Original")
pairs(PCA_results_clean$x[, 1:4], col = rgb(0, 0, 0, 0.2), pch = 16, main = "Cleaned")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# EDA for Ward variable:
PC_Summary <- data.frame(
  PCA_results_clean$x[ , 1:4],
  Ward = clean_data_no_outliers$ward_level
)

Ward_averages <- PC_Summary %>% 
  group_by(Ward) %>% 
  summarise(across(starts_with("PC"), mean, .names = "avg_col{.col}"))

# EDA for the room type:
PC_Summary$RoomType <- clean_data_no_outliers$room_type

ggpairs(PC_Summary, 
        columns = 1:4, 
        aes(color = RoomType, alpha = 0.4)) +
  theme_minimal()

# EDA for property type:
clean_data_no_outliers <- clean_data_no_outliers %>%
  mutate(property_category = case_when(
    grepl("^entire", tolower(property_type))  ~ "Entire Unit",
    grepl("^private", tolower(property_type)) ~ "Private Room",
    TRUE                                      ~ "Others"
  ))

PC_Summary$property_category <- clean_data_no_outliers$property_category

# EDA for property category themes:
eda_theme_df <- data.frame(
  availability   = availability,
  review_score   = review_score,
  listing_size   = listing_size,
  num_of_reviews = num_of_reviews,
  property_category = clean_data_no_outliers$property_category
)

eda_theme_long <- eda_theme_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -property_category, 
    names_to = "Theme", 
    values_to = "Score"
  )

# Generate the faceted boxplot with constrained Y-axis
ggplot(eda_theme_long, aes(x = property_category, y = Score, fill = property_category)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) + 
  facet_wrap(~Theme, scales = "free_y") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-5, 5)) + # This sets the view to [-1, 1]
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Airbnb Theme Distributions by Property Category",
    subtitle = "Analysis of Principal Component Scores (Focus Range)",
    y = "PC Score (Standard Deviations)", 
    x = "Category",
    fill = "Property Type"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) 

# EDA on the host_name variable:
host_freq_table <- clean_data_no_outliers %>%
  count(host_name, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    relative_freq = n / sum(n),
    percentage = relative_freq * 100
  )

head(host_freq_table, 10)

# EDA on Neighbourhood:
luxury_labels <- clean_data_no_outliers %>%
  group_by(neighbourhood) %>%
  summarise(
    lat = mean(Latitude),
    long = mean(Longitude),
    avg_price = mean(Price)
  ) %>%
  filter(avg_price > 1000) %>%

  mutate(short_name = gsub(",.*", "", neighbourhood))


ggplot(clean_data_no_outliers, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Price), alpha = 0.4, size = 0.8) + 
  scale_color_viridis(option = "magma", trans = "log10", 
                      labels = scales::dollar_format(prefix = "R")) +
  geom_text(data = luxury_labels, 
            aes(x = long, y = lat, label = short_name), # Using short_name
            size = 3, 
            check_overlap = TRUE, 
            fontface = "bold",
            color = "black",
            vjust = -0.5) + 
  theme_minimal() +

  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(18.2, 19.1), ylim = c(-34.3, -33.5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Luxury Airbnb Hubs (Avg > R1,000)",
    subtitle = "Standardized Labels for Cape Town Clusters",
    color = "Price per Night"
  )
```

\section{Part Two}

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Obtaining the variables for the clustering:
selected_variables <- c(18:29,33:39,13:17,30:32,40)
data_clust <- clean_data_no_outliers %>%
  select(selected_variables)

# Scale the data:
data_scaled <- scale(data_clust)

set.seed(123)

wss_full <- numeric(40)
wss_full[1] <- (nrow(data_scaled) - 1) * sum(apply(data_scaled, 2, var))

for (i in 2:40) {
  wss_full[i] <- sum(kmeans(data_scaled, centers = i, nstart = 20)$withinss)
}

plot(1:40, wss_full, type = "b", pch = 19, 
     xlab = "Number of Clusters", 
     ylab = "WSS",
     main = "Elbow Method: Cleaned Dataset (10,263 obs)")

data_sample <- data_scaled[sample(1:nrow(data_scaled), 2000), ]

gap_stat <- clusGap(data_sample, 
                    FUN = kmeans, 
                    nstart = 25, 
                    K.max = 15, 
                    B = 50) 

print(gap_stat, method = "firstmax") # Choose k = 6

plot(gap_stat, main = "Gap Statistic for Cape Town Airbnb Listings")

  # Compare the silhouette score of k=6 and k=7:
km6 <- kmeans(data_scaled, centers = 6, nstart = 25)
km7 <- kmeans(data_scaled, centers = 7, nstart = 25)

sil6 <- silhouette(km6$cluster, dist(data_scaled))
sil7 <- silhouette(km7$cluster, dist(data_scaled))

print(paste("Average Silhouette Width for k=6:", mean(sil6[,3])))
print(paste("Average Silhouette Width for k=7:", mean(sil7[,3])))

# Plotting the final K-Means clustering:
final_km_6 <- kmeans(data_scaled, centers = 6, nstart = 25)

base_plot_fixed <- function(pc_indices) {
  fviz_cluster(final_km_6,             
               data = PCA_results_clean$x, 
               choose.vars = pc_indices,
               geom = "point",
               ellipse = FALSE,
               show.clust.cent = TRUE,
               palette = "jco",
               ggtheme = theme_minimal()) +
    labs(x = paste0("PC", pc_indices[1]), 
         y = paste0("PC", pc_indices[2])) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(size = 10))
}

p1 <- base_plot_fixed(c(1, 2)) + labs(title = "PC1 vs PC2: Market Overview")
p2 <- base_plot_fixed(c(1, 3)) + labs(title = "PC1 vs PC3: Availability & Size")
p3 <- base_plot_fixed(c(2, 3)) + labs(title = "PC2 vs PC3: Quality & Size")
p4 <- base_plot_fixed(c(3, 4)) + labs(title = "PC3 vs PC4: The Specifics")

(p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4) + 
  plot_annotation(title = "K-Means Cluster Projection (k=6)",
                  subtitle = "Visualized across Cleaned Principal Components",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")))

# Performing Cluster Stability checks:
  
  ## Resampling or bootstrap stability:
boot_stability <- clusterboot(data_scaled, 
                              B = 100, 
                              bootmethod = "boot",
                              clustermethod = kmeansCBI, 
                              krange = 6, 
                              seed = 123)
jaccard_stability <- boot_stability$bootmean
print("Jaccard Similarity (Bootstrap Mean):")
print(jaccard_stability)

# Random initialisations:
set.seed(1)
km_run1 <- kmeans(data_scaled, centers = 6, nstart = 1)

set.seed(2)
km_run2 <- kmeans(data_scaled, centers = 6, nstart = 1)

  # Using the ARI as a comparison metric:
ari_value <- adjustedRandIndex(km_run1$cluster, km_run2$cluster)
print(paste("ARI between random starts:", round(ari_value, 4)))

  # Using the Jaccard as the comparison metric:
jaccard_random <- cl_agreement(as.cl_partition(km_run1$cluster), 
                               as.cl_partition(km_run2$cluster), 
                               method = "Jaccard")
print(paste("Jaccard Similarity between random starts:", round(jaccard_random, 4)))
```


\section{Part Three}

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
set.seed(123)

som_grid <- somgrid(xdim = 22, ydim=22, topo="hexagonal")
data_mat <- as.matrix(data_scaled)
SOM_model <- som(data_mat,grid=som_grid, rlen=1000, 
                 alpha=c(0.05,0.01), 
                 keep.data = TRUE  )
SOM_ModelCodes = som_model$codes[[1]]

# Training Process
plot(SOM_model,type="changes")

# Node Counts - Number of Observations in Each Node
plot(SOM_model, type="counts")

# U-Matrix
plot(SOM_model, type="dist.neighbours")

# Quantization Error
plot(SOM_model,type="quality")
plot(SOM_model,type="mapping")

# Individual Plots for Variables
plot(SOM_model, type="codes", palette.name = rainbow)

# Heat Maps:
plot_som_heatmap <- function(model, variable_data, title_name) {
  node_means <- aggregate(as.numeric(variable_data), 
                          by = list(model$unit.classif), 
                          FUN = mean, 
                          na.rm = TRUE)
  
  plot(model, 
       type = "property", 
       property = node_means[, 2], 
       main = title_name, 
       palette.name = viridis, 
       border = "grey")
}

par(mfrow = c(2, 2)) 

plot_som_heatmap(SOM_model, PCA_results_clean$x[,1], "Theme 1: Availability")
plot_som_heatmap(SOM_model, PCA_results_clean$x[,2], "Theme 2: Review Score")
plot_som_heatmap(SOM_model, PCA_results_clean$x[,3], "Theme 3: Listing Size")
plot_som_heatmap(SOM_model, PCA_results_clean$x[,4], "Theme 4: Num of Reviews")

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# Performing k-means on SOM:

som_codes <- SOM_model$codes[[1]]

# PERFORMING GAP STATISTIC:
gap_stat_som <- clusGap(som_codes, 
                        FUN = kmeans, 
                        nstart = 25, 
                        K.max = 15, 
                        B = 50)

fviz_gap_stat(gap_stat_som) + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = NULL) + 
  labs(title = "Gap Statistic: Finding Optimal k for SOM Nodes",
       subtitle = "Manual inspection of the elbow curve")

# SILHOUETTE SCORE:
fviz_nbclust(som_codes, kmeans, method = "silhouette", k.max = 15) +
  labs(title = "Silhouette Analysis: SOM Code Vectors",
       subtitle = "Determining cluster cohesion on the SOM grid")
km_som_6 <- kmeans(som_codes, centers = 6, nstart = 25)
km_som_13 <- kmeans(som_codes, centers = 13, nstart = 25)

# RE-RUN K-MEANS WITH THE BEST K:
best_k_som <- 6
som_cluster_results <- kmeans(som_codes, centers = best_k_som, nstart = 25)

final_cluster_assignment <- som_cluster_results$cluster[SOM_model$unit.classif]
clean_data_no_outliers$Market_Segment <- as.factor(final_cluster_assignment)

# Plot the Clusters:
plot(SOM_model, 
     type = "mapping", 
     pchs = "", 
     bgcol = viridis(best_k_som, alpha = 0.5)[som_cluster_results$cluster], 
     main = paste("Cape Town Market Segments (k =", best_k_som, ") on SOM Grid"),
     border = "grey80")

add.cluster.boundaries(SOM_model, som_cluster_results$cluster, lwd = 3)
```
